message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Migrate CI to Pixi + Add AppImage Generation

## Context

The project has 3 nearly-identical Docker-based CI workflows (ros-humble, ros-jazzy, ros-rolling) and a Docker-based release-debs workflow. Now that pixi.toml provides reproducible Humble/Jazzy environments via RoboStack, we can consolidate into a single pixi-based CI workflow. Rolling is replaced by Kilted (robostack-kilted exists, robostack-rolling does not). An experimental AppImage job is added for portable binary distribution.

## Step 1: Add Kilted environment to `pixi.toml`

**File: `pixi.toml`**

Add feature and environment:

```toml
[feature.kilted]
channels = ["robostack-kilted", "conda-forge"]

[feature.kilted.dependencies]
ros-kilted-desktop = "*"

[environments]
humble = ["humble"]
jazzy = ["jazzy"]
kilted = ["kilted"]
```

Then run `pixi install -e kilted` to update `pixi.lock`.

## Step 2: Create consolidated `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`

Replace all 3 Docker-based workflows with one pixi-based workflow:

```yaml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [development, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [development, main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy, kilted]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: ${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true
          cache-write: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref_name == 'main' }}

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Test
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} test
```

Key design choices:
- **`locked: true`** — enforces committed pixi.lock (reproducible)
- **`cache: true`** — caches pixi environments from lockfile hash
- **`cache-write` only on main push** — avoids polluting cache from PR branches
- **No Docker containers** — pixi provides the full toolchain
- **`ubuntu-latest`** — runner OS doesn't matter since pixi provides everything

## Step 3: Delete old CI workflows

Delete:
- `.github/workflows/ros-humble.yaml`
- `.github/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml`
- `.github/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml`

## Step 4: Add experimental AppImage job to `ci.yaml`

Add a job that builds an AppImage for `pj_bridge_ros2`, triggered only on tags or manual dispatch:

```yaml
  appimage:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: humble
          locked: true
          cache: true

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e humble build

      - name: Create AppImage
        run: |
          # Download tools
          wget -q https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy/releases/download/continuous/linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage
          chmod +x linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage

          CONDA_PREFIX="$(pixi run -e humble printenv CONDA_PREFIX)"

          # Create AppDir structure
          mkdir -p AppDir/usr/{bin,lib,share}

          # Copy binary
          cp install/lib/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_ros2 AppDir/usr/bin/

          # Bundle shared libraries from the conda environment
          # (exclude glibc/ld-linux which must come from the host)
          ldd AppDir/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2 | \
            grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
            awk '{print $3}' | \
            xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Also copy transitive deps from conda lib/
          for lib in AppDir/usr/lib/*.so*; do
            ldd "$lib" 2>/dev/null | \
              grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
              awk '{print $3}' | \
              xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Copy ament index (needed for schema discovery at runtime)
          cp -r "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/ament_index AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true
          # Copy message definitions (.msg/.srv/.idl files)
          for pkg_dir in "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/msg "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/srv; do
            [ -d "$pkg_dir" ] && cp -r --parents "$pkg_dir" AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Create .desktop file
          cat > AppDir/pj_bridge.desktop <<'DESKTOP'
          [Desktop Entry]
          Name=pj_bridge
          Exec=pj_bridge_ros2
          Icon=pj_bridge
          Type=Application
          Categories=Science;
          DESKTOP

          # Create minimal icon
          convert -size 256x256 xc:transparent AppDir/pj_bridge.png 2>/dev/null || \
            touch AppDir/pj_bridge.png

          # Create AppRun wrapper that sets environment
          cat > AppDir/AppRun <<'APPRUN'
          #!/bin/bash
          SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
          HERE="${SELF%/*}"
          export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${HERE}/usr/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
          export AMENT_PREFIX_PATH="${HERE}/usr"
          exec "${HERE}/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2" "$@"
          APPRUN
          chmod +x AppDir/AppRun

          # Generate AppImage
          ARCH=x86_64 ./linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage \
            --appdir AppDir \
            --output appimage \
            || appimagetool AppDir/  # fallback

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: appimage-humble
          path: "*.AppImage"
```

**Note**: This is experimental. The library bundling and ament_index copying may need iteration. The AppImage will be large (~200-500 MB) since it includes ROS2 shared libs.

## Step 5: Keep `release-debs.yaml` Docker-based

No changes. Bloom/dpkg needs system ROS packages and Debian packaging tools. Building .debs inside pixi would produce binaries linked against conda libs, which won't work on standard Ubuntu+ROS systems. Update the matrix to remove `rolling` and keep `humble`, `jazzy`, `kilted`.

---

## Files modified

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `pixi.toml` | Add `kilted` feature and environment |
| `pixi.lock` | Regenerate (after adding kilted) |
| `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` | Create (consolidated pixi CI + AppImage) |
| `.github/workflows/ros-humble.yaml` | Delete |
| `.github/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml` | Delete |
| `.github/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml` | Delete |
| `.github/workflows/release-debs.yaml` | Update matrix: drop rolling, add kilted |

## Verification

1. Push to a branch and verify the `ci.yaml` workflow runs for all 3 environments (humble, jazzy, kilted)
2. Trigger manually to test the AppImage job
3. Verify the `.deb` release workflow still works (unchanged fundamentally)


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/davide/.REDACTED.jsonl

**File: `pixi.toml`**

Add feature and environment:

```toml
[feature.kilted]
channels = ["robostack-kilted", "conda-forge"]

[feature.kilted.dependencies]

humble = ["humble"]

## Step 2: Create consolidated `.github/workflows/ci.yaml`

```yaml
name: CI

  push:
    branches: [development, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [development, main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

  build-and-test:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false

        environment: [humble, jazzy, kilted]

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

- **`locked: true`** — enforces committed pixi.lock (reproducible)

- `.github/workflows/ros-humble.yaml`
- `.github/workflows/ros-jazzy.yaml`
- `.github/workflows/ros-rolling.yaml`

```yaml

        run: pixi run -e humble build

| File | Action |
|------|--------|

| `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` | Create (consolidated pixi CI + AppImage) |

[workspace]
name = "pj_bridge"
channels = ["conda-forge"]
platforms = ["linux-64"]

[dependencies]
cmake = ">=3.14"
compilers = "*"
pkg-config = "*"
ninja = "*"
colcon-common-extensions = "*"
zstd = "*"
spdlog = "*"
ixwebsocket = "*"
nlohmann_json = "*"

[feature.humble]
channels = ["robostack-humble", "conda-forge"]

[feature.humble.dependencies]
ros-humble-desktop = "*"

[feature.jazzy]
channels = ["robostack-jazzy", "conda-forge"]

[feature.jazzy.dependencies]
ros-jazzy-desktop = "*"

[environments]
humble = ["humble"]
jazzy = ["jazzy"]

[activation]
scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]

[tasks]
build = "colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release"
test = "colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose"
clean = "rm -rf build install log"

file.content:
name: ROS2 Humble

on:
  push:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    container:
      image: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-jammy-ros-humble-ros-base-latest
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros
    steps:
      # Checkout repository
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Setup ccache for faster rebuilds
      - name: Setup ccache
        uses: hendrikmuhs/ccache-action@v1.2
        with:
          key: ${{ github.job }}-humble
          max-size: 500M

      # Configure ccache for colcon
      - name: Configure ccache
        run: echo "/usr/lib/ccache" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      # Install dependencies via rosdep
      - name: Install dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
          apt-get update
          rosdep update --rosdistro humble
          rosdep install --from-paths . --ignore-src -y --rosdistro humble

      # Build
      - name: Build
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
          colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge \
            --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache \
            -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache

      # Test
      - name: Test
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash
          source install/setup.bash
          colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge
          colcon test-result --verbose

file.content:
name: ROS2 Jazzy

on:
  push:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    container:
      image: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-jazzy-ros-base-latest
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros
    steps:
      # Checkout repository
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Setup ccache for faster rebuilds
      - name: Setup ccache
        uses: hendrikmuhs/ccache-action@v1.2
        with:
          key: ${{ github.job }}-jazzy
          max-size: 500M

      # Configure ccache for colcon
      - name: Configure ccache
        run: echo "/usr/lib/ccache" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      # Install dependencies via rosdep
      - name: Install dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/jazzy/setup.bash
          apt-get update
          rosdep update --rosdistro jazzy
          rosdep install --from-paths . --ignore-src -y --rosdistro jazzy

      # Build
      - name: Build
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/jazzy/setup.bash
          colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge \
            --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache \

file.content:
name: ROS2 Rolling

on:
  push:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ development, main ]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    container:
      image: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-rolling-ros-base-latest
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros
    steps:
      # Checkout repository
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Setup ccache for faster rebuilds
      - name: Setup ccache
        uses: hendrikmuhs/ccache-action@v1.2
        with:
          key: ${{ github.job }}-rolling
          max-size: 500M

      # Configure ccache for colcon
      - name: Configure ccache
        run: echo "/usr/lib/ccache" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      # Install dependencies via rosdep
      - name: Install dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash
          apt-get update
          rosdep update --rosdistro rolling
          rosdep install --from-paths . --ignore-src -y --rosdistro rolling

      # Build
      - name: Build
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash
          colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge \
            --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
            -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache \
            -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_LAUNCHER=ccache

      # Test
      - name: Test
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/rolling/setup.bash
          source install/setup.bash
          colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge
          colcon test-result --verbose

file.content:
name: Build & Release Debian Packages

on:
  push:
    tags: ['*']
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-deb:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - ros_distro: humble
            os_codename: jammy
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-jammy-ros-humble-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-22.04
          - ros_distro: jazzy
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-jazzy-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04
          - ros_distro: kilted
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-kilted-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04
          - ros_distro: rolling
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-rolling-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    container:
      image: ${{ matrix.container }}
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          path: src/pj_bridge

      - name: Install build tools
        run: |
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y python3-bloom fakeroot debhelper dh-python

      - name: Install ROS dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          rosdep update --rosdistro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}
          rosdep install --from-paths src/pj_bridge --ignore-src -y --rosdistro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}

      - name: Clean previous builds
        shell: bash
        run: |
          cd src/pj_bridge
          rm -rf debian .obj-x86_64-linux-gnu

      - name: Generate debian packaging
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          cd src/pj_bridge
          bloom-generate rosdebian \
            --os-name ubuntu \
            --os-version ${{ matrix.os_codename }} \
            --ros-distro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}

      - name: Build debian package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          cd src/pj_bridge
          fakeroot debian/rules binary

      - name: Upload deb artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: deb-${{ matrix.ros_distro }}
          path: src/ros-${{ matrix.ros_distro }}-*.deb

  release:
    needs: build-deb
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Determine release tag
        id: tag
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.ref_type }}" == "tag" ]]; then
            echo "name=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "prerelease=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            LATEST_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "0.0.0")
            echo "name=${LATEST_TAG}-dev" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "prerelease=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Download all deb artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: debs
          pattern: deb-*
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: List artifacts
        run: ls -lh debs/

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.name }}
          name: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.name }}
          prerelease: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.prerelease }}
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: debs/*.deb

Base directory for this skill: /home/davide/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.0.3/skills/executing-plans

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute tasks in batches, report for review between batches.

**Core principle:** Batch execution with checkpoints for architect review.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Batch
**Default: First 3 tasks**

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Report
When batch complete:
- Show what was implemented
- Show verification output
- Say: "Ready for feedback."

### Step 4: Continue
Based on feedback:
- Apply changes if needed
- Execute next batch
- Repeat until complete

### Step 5: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker mid-batch (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Between batches: just report and wait
- Stop when blocked, don't guess

Based on the search results, here's what I found about RoboStack's kilted conda channel and ROS 2:

## RoboStack and ROS 2 Kilted

RoboStack provides a ros-kilted conda channel that allows users to create a ros-kilted desktop environment using conda. RoboStack is a bundling of ROS for Linux, macOS and Windows using the Conda package manager, based on top of conda-forge.

## Installation

Users can create a ros-kilted desktop environment using either conda or micromamba by specifying the robostack-kilted channel. The documentation recommends using Pixi for any new installations.

## ROS 2 Support

RoboStack provides ROS packages for ROS1 Noetic and ROS2 Humble, as well as unsupported Melodic/Foxy/Galactic packages. Additionally, RoboStack provides ROS binaries for Linux, macOS, Windows and ARM (Linux).

## Community Project Status

Although conda packages for ROS 2 may be built by the community-maintained RoboStack project, no official conda packages for ROS 2 are provided by Open Robotics.

The search results don't contain specific information about 2025-2026 release plans or timeline updates, but they do confirm that the robostack-kilted channel is actively maintained for ROS 2 Kilted support.

I'll proceed with all the file changes and we'll regenerate `pixi.lock` when the channels come back. The CI workflow uses `locked: true` which requires the lock file, but we can update that once channels are available.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [development, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [development, main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy, kilted]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: ${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true
          cache-write: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref_name == 'main' }}

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Test
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} test

  appimage:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy, kilted]
    name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Create AppImage
        run: |
          # Download linuxdeploy
          wget -q https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy/releases/download/continuous/linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage
          chmod +x linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage

          CONDA_PREFIX="$(pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} printenv CONDA_PREFIX)"

          # Create AppDir structure
          mkdir -p AppDir/usr/{bin,lib,share}

          # Copy binary
          cp install/lib/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_ros2 AppDir/usr/bin/

          # Bundle shared libraries from the conda environment
          # (exclude glibc/ld-linux which must come from the host)
          ldd AppDir/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2 | \
            grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
            awk '{print $3}' | \
            xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Also copy transitive deps from conda lib/
          for lib in AppDir/usr/lib/*.so*; do
            ldd "$lib" 2>/dev/null | \
              grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
              awk '{print $3}' | \
              xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Copy ament index (needed for schema discovery at runtime)
          cp -r "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/ament_index AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Copy message definitions (.msg/.srv/.idl files)
          for pkg_dir in "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/msg "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/srv; do
            [ -d "$pkg_dir" ] && cp -r --parents "$pkg_dir" AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Create .desktop file
          cat > AppDir/pj_bridge.desktop <<'DESKTOP'
          [Desktop Entry]
          Name=pj_bridge
          Exec=pj_bridge_ros2
          Icon=pj_bridge
          Type=Application
          Categories=Science;
          DESKTOP

          # Create minimal icon
          convert -size 256x256 xc:transparent AppDir/pj_bridge.png 2>/dev/null || \
            touch AppDir/pj_bridge.png

          # Create AppRun wrapper that sets environment
          cat > AppDir/AppRun <<'APPRUN'
          #!/bin/bash
          SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
          HERE="${SELF%/*}"
          export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${HERE}/usr/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
          export AMENT_PREFIX_PATH="${HERE}/usr"
          exec "${HERE}/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2" "$@"
          APPRUN
          chmod +x AppDir/AppRun

          # Generate AppImage
          ARCH=x86_64 ./linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage \
            --appdir AppDir \
            --output appimage

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}
          path: "*.AppImage"

name: Build & Release Debian Packages

on:
  push:
    tags: ['*']
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-deb:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - ros_distro: humble
            os_codename: jammy
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-jammy-ros-humble-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-22.04
          - ros_distro: jazzy
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-jazzy-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04
          - ros_distro: kilted
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-kilted-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04
          - ros_distro: rolling
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-rolling-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    container:
      image: ${{ matrix.container }}
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          path: src/pj_bridge

      - name: Install build tools
        run: |
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y python3-bloom fakeroot debhelper dh-python

      - name: Install ROS dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          rosdep update --rosdistro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}
          rosdep install --from-paths src/pj_bridge --ignore-src -y --rosdistro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}

      - name: Clean previous builds
        shell: bash
        run: |
          cd src/pj_bridge
          rm -rf debian .obj-x86_64-linux-gnu

      - name: Generate debian packaging
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          cd src/pj_bridge
          bloom-generate rosdebian \
            --os-name ubuntu \
            --os-version ${{ matrix.os_codename }} \
            --ros-distro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}

      - name: Build debian package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          cd src/pj_bridge
          fakeroot debian/rules binary

      - name: Upload deb artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: deb-${{ matrix.ros_distro }}
          path: src/ros-${{ matrix.ros_distro }}-*.deb

  release:
    needs: build-deb
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Determine release tag
        id: tag
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.ref_type }}" == "tag" ]]; then
            echo "name=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "prerelease=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            LATEST_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "0.0.0")
            echo "name=${LATEST_TAG}-dev" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "prerelease=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Download all deb artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: debs
          pattern: deb-*
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: List artifacts
        run: ls -lh debs/

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.name }}
          name: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.name }}
          prerelease: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.prerelease }}
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: debs/*.deb

name: Build & Release Debian Packages

on:
  push:
    tags: ['*']
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-deb:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - ros_distro: humble
            os_codename: jammy
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-jammy-ros-humble-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-22.04
          - ros_distro: jazzy
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-jazzy-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04
          - ros_distro: kilted
            os_codename: noble
            container: rostooling/setup-ros-docker:ubuntu-noble-ros-kilted-ros-base-latest
            runner: ubuntu-24.04

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    container:
      image: ${{ matrix.container }}
    env:
      HOME: /root
      ROS_HOME: /root/.ros

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          path: src/pj_bridge

      - name: Install build tools
        run: |
          apt-get update
          apt-get install -y python3-bloom fakeroot debhelper dh-python

      - name: Install ROS dependencies
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          rosdep update --rosdistro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}
          rosdep install --from-paths src/pj_bridge --ignore-src -y --rosdistro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}

      - name: Clean previous builds
        shell: bash
        run: |
          cd src/pj_bridge
          rm -rf debian .obj-x86_64-linux-gnu

      - name: Generate debian packaging
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          cd src/pj_bridge
          bloom-generate rosdebian \
            --os-name ubuntu \
            --os-version ${{ matrix.os_codename }} \
            --ros-distro ${{ matrix.ros_distro }}

      - name: Build debian package
        shell: bash
        run: |
          source /opt/ros/${{ matrix.ros_distro }}/setup.bash
          cd src/pj_bridge
          fakeroot debian/rules binary

      - name: Upload deb artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: deb-${{ matrix.ros_distro }}
          path: src/ros-${{ matrix.ros_distro }}-*.deb

  release:
    needs: build-deb
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Determine release tag
        id: tag
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.ref_type }}" == "tag" ]]; then
            echo "name=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "prerelease=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            LATEST_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "0.0.0")
            echo "name=${LATEST_TAG}-dev" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "prerelease=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Download all deb artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: debs
          pattern: deb-*
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: List artifacts
        run: ls -lh debs/

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.name }}
          name: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.name }}
          prerelease: ${{ steps.tag.outputs.prerelease }}
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: debs/*.deb

file.content:
exclude: ^.vscode/|^deps/|^build/|^3rdparty/
repos:

  # Standard hooks
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v4.5.0
    hooks:
      - id: check-added-large-files
      - id: check-ast
      - id: check-case-conflict
      - id: check-merge-conflict
      - id: check-symlinks
      - id: check-xml
      - id: check-yaml
        exclude: .gitlab-ci.yml
      - id: debug-statements
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
        exclude_types: [svg]
      - id: mixed-line-ending
      - id: trailing-whitespace
        exclude_types: [svg]
      - id: fix-byte-order-marker

  # CPP hooks
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/mirrors-clang-format
    rev: v17.0.6
    hooks:
      - id: clang-format
        types_or: [c++, c]
        args: ['-fallback-style=none', '-i']

content.text: I'll add `args: ['--maxkb=1024']` to the `check-added-large-files` hook to allow files up to 1 MB (pixi.lock is 811 KB):

toolUseResult.originalFile:
exclude: ^.vscode/|^deps/|^build/|^3rdparty/
repos:

  # Standard hooks
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit-hooks
    rev: v4.5.0
    hooks:
      - id: check-added-large-files
      - id: check-ast
      - id: check-case-conflict
      - id: check-merge-conflict
      - id: check-symlinks
      - id: check-xml
      - id: check-yaml
        exclude: .gitlab-ci.yml
      - id: debug-statements
      - id: end-of-file-fixer
        exclude_types: [svg]
      - id: mixed-line-ending
      - id: trailing-whitespace
        exclude_types: [svg]
      - id: fix-byte-order-marker

  # CPP hooks
  - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/mirrors-clang-format
    rev: v17.0.6
    hooks:
      - id: clang-format
        types_or: [c++, c]
        args: ['-fallback-style=none', '-i']

input.command:
gh pr create --title "ci: Migrate to pixi-based CI with AppImage generation" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Replace 3 Docker-based CI workflows (ros-humble, ros-jazzy, ros-rolling) with a single pixi-based `ci.yaml`
- Matrix over `humble`, `jazzy`, `kilted` environments using RoboStack conda channels
- Add experimental AppImage generation job (one per ROS distro), triggered on tags or manual dispatch
- Drop `rolling` from `release-debs.yaml` matrix (robostack-rolling doesn't exist; kilted already present)
- Bump pre-commit `check-added-large-files` limit to 1 MB for `pixi.lock`

## Known issue
- The `kilted` environment is not yet in `pixi.lock` — the `robostack-kilted` channel was returning 503 during development. The lock needs to be regenerated once the channel is back. The `kilted` CI job will fail until then.

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `humble` and `jazzy` CI jobs pass
- [ ] Verify `kilted` CI job fails with clear lock error (expected until lock is regenerated)
- [ ] Trigger manual dispatch to test AppImage generation
- [ ] Verify `release-debs.yaml` matrix no longer includes `rolling`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" --base main 2>&1

content.thinking:
The error is: `lock-file not up-to-date with the workspace`. This makes sense - the pixi.toml has the kilted environment, but the lock file doesn't include it (since we couldn't resolve it due to the robostack outage). So pixi's `--locked` flag sees a mismatch between the manifest and lock file.

Two options:
1. Remove the kilted environment from pixi.toml until we can regenerate the lock (this will let humble and jazzy pass)
2. Use `locked: false` in the CI (defeats the purpose of reproducibility)

Option 1 is the right approach. Let me remove kilted from pixi.toml for now. Actually wait, let me think about this differently. 

The lock was generated before I added kilted, so it only has default, humble, and jazzy. Now that pixi.toml declares kilted too, the `--locked` flag is rejecting the mismatch. I could regenerate the lock with kilted included, but robostack is down. The setup-pixi action has a `frozen` option that installs only from the lock file without validating it's up-to-date - that might be the way to go instead of removing kilted.

Actually, the real issue is that pixi validates the entire manifest against the lock file regardless of which environment I'm installing. So even if I only use humble or jazzy, all three CI jobs fail because kilted is missing from the lock. The cleanest solution is to remove kilted from both pixi.toml and the CI matrix for now, then add them both back once robostack is operational.

content.text: The error is `lock-file not up-to-date with the workspace` — pixi's `--locked` flag rejects the mismatch between pixi.toml (has kilted) and pixi.lock (missing kilted). Since robostack channels are down, I need to remove kilted from pixi.toml to match the existing lock. I'll keep it in the CI yaml commented so it's easy to add back.

input.new_string:
# Uncomment when robostack-kilted channel is available and regenerate pixi.lock:
# [feature.kilted]
# channels = ["robostack-kilted", "conda-forge"]
#
# [feature.kilted.dependencies]
# ros-kilted-desktop = "*"

[workspace]
name = "pj_bridge"
channels = ["conda-forge"]
platforms = ["linux-64"]

[dependencies]
cmake = ">=3.14"
compilers = "*"
pkg-config = "*"
ninja = "*"
colcon-common-extensions = "*"
zstd = "*"
spdlog = "*"
ixwebsocket = "*"
nlohmann_json = "*"

[feature.humble]
channels = ["robostack-humble", "conda-forge"]

[feature.humble.dependencies]
ros-humble-desktop = "*"

[feature.jazzy]
channels = ["robostack-jazzy", "conda-forge"]

[feature.jazzy.dependencies]
ros-jazzy-desktop = "*"

[feature.kilted]
channels = ["robostack-kilted", "conda-forge"]

[feature.kilted.dependencies]
ros-kilted-desktop = "*"

[environments]
humble = ["humble"]
jazzy = ["jazzy"]
kilted = ["kilted"]

[activation]
scripts = ["install/setup.sh"]

[tasks]
build = "colcon build --packages-select pj_bridge --cmake-args -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release"
test = "colcon test --packages-select pj_bridge && colcon test-result --verbose"
clean = "rm -rf build install log"

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [development, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [development, main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy, kilted]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: ${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true
          cache-write: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref_name == 'main' }}

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Test
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} test

  appimage:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy, kilted]
    name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Create AppImage
        run: |
          # Download linuxdeploy
          wget -q https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy/releases/download/continuous/linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage
          chmod +x linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage

          CONDA_PREFIX="$(pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} printenv CONDA_PREFIX)"

          # Create AppDir structure
          mkdir -p AppDir/usr/{bin,lib,share}

          # Copy binary
          cp install/lib/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_ros2 AppDir/usr/bin/

          # Bundle shared libraries from the conda environment
          # (exclude glibc/ld-linux which must come from the host)
          ldd AppDir/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2 | \
            grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
            awk '{print $3}' | \
            xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Also copy transitive deps from conda lib/
          for lib in AppDir/usr/lib/*.so*; do
            ldd "$lib" 2>/dev/null | \
              grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
              awk '{print $3}' | \
              xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Copy ament index (needed for schema discovery at runtime)
          cp -r "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/ament_index AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Copy message definitions (.msg/.srv/.idl files)
          for pkg_dir in "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/msg "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/srv; do
            [ -d "$pkg_dir" ] && cp -r --parents "$pkg_dir" AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Create .desktop file
          cat > AppDir/pj_bridge.desktop <<'DESKTOP'
          [Desktop Entry]
          Name=pj_bridge
          Exec=pj_bridge_ros2
          Icon=pj_bridge
          Type=Application
          Categories=Science;
          DESKTOP

          # Create minimal icon
          convert -size 256x256 xc:transparent AppDir/pj_bridge.png 2>/dev/null || \
            touch AppDir/pj_bridge.png

          # Create AppRun wrapper that sets environment
          cat > AppDir/AppRun <<'APPRUN'
          #!/bin/bash
          SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
          HERE="${SELF%/*}"
          export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${HERE}/usr/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
          export AMENT_PREFIX_PATH="${HERE}/usr"
          exec "${HERE}/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2" "$@"
          APPRUN
          chmod +x AppDir/AppRun

          # Generate AppImage
          ARCH=x86_64 ./linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage \
            --appdir AppDir \
            --output appimage

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}
          path: "*.AppImage"

      - name: Create AppImage
        run: |

          CONDA_PREFIX="$(pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} printenv CONDA_PREFIX)"

          cp install/lib/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_ros2 AppDir/usr/bin/

          done

          # Create .desktop file
          cat > AppDir/pj_bridge.desktop <<'DESKTOP'
          [Desktop Entry]
          Name=pj_bridge
          Exec=pj_bridge_ros2
          Icon=pj_bridge
          Type=Application
          Categories=Science;
          DESKTOP

          # Create minimal icon
          convert -size 256x256 xc:transparent AppDir/pj_bridge.png 2>/dev/null || \
            touch AppDir/pj_bridge.png

          # Create AppRun wrapper that sets environment
          cat > AppDir/AppRun <<'APPRUN'
          #!/bin/bash
          SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
          HERE="${SELF%/*}"
          export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${HERE}/usr/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
          export AMENT_PREFIX_PATH="${HERE}/usr"
          exec "${HERE}/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2" "$@"
          APPRUN
          chmod +x AppDir/AppRun

          # Generate AppImage
          ARCH=x86_64 ./linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage \

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4

          path: "*.AppImage"

All robostack channels are down (503) - not just kilted, but humble and jazzy too! The error is:

```
HTTP status server error (503 Service Unavailable) for url (https://conda.anaconda.org/robostack-humble/linux-64/ros-humble-lifecycle-0.20.5-np126py311hbc2a38a_13.conda)
```

So the entire robostack infrastructure on anaconda.org is experiencing an outage. All pixi-based CI will fail until robostack is back up.

This is an infrastructure outage, not a bug in our workflow. 

The workflow itself is functioning correctly - it properly identified the packages and attempted to fetch them, but the server's unavailability is blocking everything. There's nothing to fix on our end; we just need to wait for robostack to recover.

content.text:
The failure is **not in our workflow** — it's the robostack anaconda.org infrastructure. All robostack channels (humble, jazzy, kilted) are returning 503. The error:

```
HTTP status server error (503 Service Unavailable) for url
(https://conda.anaconda.org/robostack-humble/linux-64/ros-humble-lifecycle-0.20.5-...)
```

The workflow itself is correct — pixi found the right packages from the lock file and attempted to download them. The server is just down. This is an upstream outage affecting all of robostack on anaconda.org.

Let me update the task and PR:

## CI Status

Both `humble` and `jazzy` jobs failed due to **robostack anaconda.org outage** (503 Service Unavailable on all robostack channels). This is an upstream infrastructure issue, not a workflow bug.

The workflow correctly:
1. Installs pixi
2. Reads the manifest and lock file
3. Attempts to download packages from lock

The server returned 503 when fetching packages like `ros-humble-lifecycle-0.20.5-...conda`.

**Action**: Re-run CI once robostack channels are back online.
EOF
)" 2>&1

### CI status:
Both humble and jazzy failed due to an **anaconda.org/robostack outage** (all robostack channels returning 503). The workflow logic is correct — just needs a re-run once the channels are back.

1. Run `pixi install -e kilted` to regenerate `pixi.lock` with kilted
2. Uncomment kilted in `pixi.toml` and `ci.yaml` matrices
3. Re-run CI to verify all 3 environments pass

for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  status=$(curl -sI "https://conda.anaconda.org/robostack-kilted/linux-64/repodata.json" -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}")
  echo "$(date +%H:%M:%S) Attempt $i: HTTP $status"
  if [ "$status" = "200" ]; then
    echo "Channel is back!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 30
done

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [development, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [development, main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy]  # Add kilted once pixi.lock is regenerated
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: ${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true
          cache-write: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref_name == 'main' }}

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Test
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} test

  appimage:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy]  # Add kilted once pixi.lock is regenerated
    name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Create AppImage
        run: |
          # Download linuxdeploy
          wget -q https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy/releases/download/continuous/linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage
          chmod +x linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage

          CONDA_PREFIX="$(pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} printenv CONDA_PREFIX)"

          # Create AppDir structure
          mkdir -p AppDir/usr/{bin,lib,share}

          # Copy binary
          cp install/lib/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_ros2 AppDir/usr/bin/

          # Bundle shared libraries from the conda environment
          # (exclude glibc/ld-linux which must come from the host)
          ldd AppDir/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2 | \
            grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
            awk '{print $3}' | \
            xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Also copy transitive deps from conda lib/
          for lib in AppDir/usr/lib/*.so*; do
            ldd "$lib" 2>/dev/null | \
              grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
              awk '{print $3}' | \
              xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Copy ament index (needed for schema discovery at runtime)
          cp -r "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/ament_index AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Copy message definitions (.msg/.srv/.idl files)
          for pkg_dir in "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/msg "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/srv; do
            [ -d "$pkg_dir" ] && cp -r --parents "$pkg_dir" AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Create .desktop file
          cat > AppDir/pj_bridge.desktop <<'DESKTOP'
          [Desktop Entry]
          Name=pj_bridge
          Exec=pj_bridge_ros2
          Icon=pj_bridge
          Type=Application
          Categories=Science;
          DESKTOP

          # Create minimal icon
          convert -size 256x256 xc:transparent AppDir/pj_bridge.png 2>/dev/null || \
            touch AppDir/pj_bridge.png

          # Create AppRun wrapper that sets environment
          cat > AppDir/AppRun <<'APPRUN'
          #!/bin/bash
          SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
          HERE="${SELF%/*}"
          export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${HERE}/usr/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
          export AMENT_PREFIX_PATH="${HERE}/usr"
          exec "${HERE}/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2" "$@"
          APPRUN
          chmod +x AppDir/AppRun

          # Generate AppImage
          ARCH=x86_64 ./linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage \
            --appdir AppDir \
            --output appimage

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}
          path: "*.AppImage"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [development, main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [development, main]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy]  # Add kilted once pixi.lock is regenerated
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    name: ${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true
          cache-write: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref_name == 'main' }}

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Test
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} test

  appimage:
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/') || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        environment: [humble, jazzy]  # Add kilted once pixi.lock is regenerated
    name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: prefix-dev/setup-pixi@v0.9.4
        with:
          environments: ${{ matrix.environment }}
          locked: true
          cache: true

      - name: Build
        run: pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} build

      - name: Create AppImage
        run: |
          # Download linuxdeploy
          wget -q https://github.com/linuxdeploy/linuxdeploy/releases/download/continuous/linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage
          chmod +x linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage

          CONDA_PREFIX="$(pixi run -e ${{ matrix.environment }} printenv CONDA_PREFIX)"

          # Create AppDir structure
          mkdir -p AppDir/usr/{bin,lib,share}

          # Copy binary
          cp install/lib/pj_bridge/pj_bridge_ros2 AppDir/usr/bin/

          # Bundle shared libraries from the conda environment
          # (exclude glibc/ld-linux which must come from the host)
          ldd AppDir/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2 | \
            grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
            awk '{print $3}' | \
            xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Also copy transitive deps from conda lib/
          for lib in AppDir/usr/lib/*.so*; do
            ldd "$lib" 2>/dev/null | \
              grep "$CONDA_PREFIX" | \
              awk '{print $3}' | \
              xargs -I{} cp --no-dereference {} AppDir/usr/lib/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Copy ament index (needed for schema discovery at runtime)
          cp -r "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/ament_index AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true

          # Copy message definitions (.msg/.srv/.idl files)
          for pkg_dir in "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/msg "$CONDA_PREFIX"/share/*/srv; do
            [ -d "$pkg_dir" ] && cp -r --parents "$pkg_dir" AppDir/usr/share/ 2>/dev/null || true
          done

          # Create .desktop file
          cat > AppDir/pj_bridge.desktop <<'DESKTOP'
          [Desktop Entry]
          Name=pj_bridge
          Exec=pj_bridge_ros2
          Icon=pj_bridge
          Type=Application
          Categories=Science;
          DESKTOP

          # Create minimal icon
          convert -size 256x256 xc:transparent AppDir/pj_bridge.png 2>/dev/null || \
            touch AppDir/pj_bridge.png

          # Create AppRun wrapper that sets environment
          cat > AppDir/AppRun <<'APPRUN'
          #!/bin/bash
          SELF="$(readlink -f "$0")"
          HERE="${SELF%/*}"
          export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="${HERE}/usr/lib:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH}"
          export AMENT_PREFIX_PATH="${HERE}/usr"
          exec "${HERE}/usr/bin/pj_bridge_ros2" "$@"
          APPRUN
          chmod +x AppDir/AppRun

          # Generate AppImage
          ARCH=x86_64 ./linuxdeploy-x86_64.AppImage \
            --appdir AppDir \
            --output appimage

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: appimage-${{ matrix.environment }}
          path: "*.AppImage"

content.text:
All 3 environments (humble, jazzy, kilted) are running. Let me answer your question while CI runs.

Looking at the pixi.toml dependencies:

```toml
cmake, compilers, pkg-config, ninja  # build tools
colcon-common-extensions             # ROS build tool
zstd, spdlog, nlohmann_json          # in rosdep / system packages
ixwebsocket                          # NOT in rosdep
```

**Yes, `ixwebsocket` is the only runtime dependency not in rosdep/rosdistro.** The others (`zstd`, `spdlog`, `nlohmann_json`) all have rosdep keys mapping to system packages. `ixwebsocket` is fetched via FetchContent in CMake for the Debian builds and via pixi/conda for the pixi builds.

Let me check CI progress:

content: do you think that is worth considering a transition to websocketpp? this is available on rosdistro, pixi and conan

content.content: Command was manually backgrounded by user with ID: b0ae1522z. Output is being written to: REDACTED.output

content.thinking:
The user is asking about transitioning from IXWebSocket to websocketpp. Let me think about this:

IXWebSocket vs websocketpp:

**IXWebSocket** (current):
- Modern C++11/14, actively maintained
- Built-in TLS, compression, auto-reconnect
- Simple API (callbacks)
- Not in rosdep/rosdistro - this is the packaging pain point

**websocketpp**:
- Available in rosdep, pixi/conda, and conan
- Uses Boost.Asio (or standalone Asio)
- Header-only option available
- Widely used in ROS ecosystem (rosbridge, etc.)


- Less actively maintained, more complex API, but would eliminate the FetchContent dependency entirely

The real question is whether the packaging convenience outweighs the added complexity. Since the WebSocket usage here is fairly basic—just a server handling text frames and binary frames—the extra boilerplate from websocketpp might be worth it to avoid the rosdep packaging headaches.

content.text:
That's a reasonable consideration. Here's the tradeoff:

**Pros of switching to websocketpp:**
- Eliminates the last non-rosdep dependency — cleaner Debian packaging (no FetchContent workaround)
- Available everywhere: rosdep, conda-forge, conan
- Header-only option available
- Well-established in the ROS ecosystem (rosbridge uses it)

**Cons:**
- More verbose API — IXWebSocket's callback-based server is very concise for what we need
- websocketpp's API is lower-level (requires more boilerplate for connection management, frame types)
- Less actively maintained than IXWebSocket
- Depends on Boost.Asio (or standalone Asio) — another dependency, though Boost is already in rosdistro
- The `WebSocketMiddleware` class would need a full rewrite

**My take:** It's worth it *if* packaging simplicity is a priority. The code impact is limited to `WebSocketMiddleware` (~200 lines) since it's already behind the `MiddlewareInterface` abstraction. The rest of the codebase wouldn't change. But it's not urgent — the FetchContent approach works fine for now.

file.content:
# Copyright (c) Meta Platforms, Inc. and affiliates.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); you may not
# use this file except in compliance with the License. You may obtain a copy of
# the License at
#
# http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT
# WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the
# License for the specific language governing permissions and limitations under
# the License.

#
# * Try to find Facebook zstd library This will define ZSTD_FOUND
#   ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR ZSTD_LIBRARY
#

if(UNIX)
  find_package(PkgConfig QUIET)
  pkg_search_module(PC_ZSTD libzstd)
endif()

find_path(ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR zstd.h
  HINTS
    ${PC_ZSTD_INCLUDEDIR}
    ${PC_ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIRS}
)

find_library(ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY
  NAMES ${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_PREFIX}zstd${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}
  HINTS
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBDIR}
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBRARY_DIRS}
)

find_library(ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY
  NAMES ${CMAKE_STATIC_LIBRARY_PREFIX}zstd${CMAKE_STATIC_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}
  HINTS
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBDIR}
    ${PC_ZSTD_LIBRARY_DIRS}
)

# Some environments (e.g. conda/conda-forge, Pixi) may not ship the static library.
# Use the shared library as a fallback so consumers can still link successfully.
if(NOT ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY AND ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY)
  set(ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY "${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}")
endif()

include(FindPackageHandleStandardArgs)
find_package_handle_standard_args(ZSTD
  DEFAULT_MSG
  ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR
  ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY
)

if(ZSTD_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "Found Zstd: shared=${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}, static=${ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY}")
else()
  message(STATUS "ZSTD not found")
  return()
endif()

mark_as_advanced(ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)

# Create imported targets
if(NOT TARGET zstd::libzstd_shared AND ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY)
  add_library(zstd::libzstd_shared SHARED IMPORTED GLOBAL)
  set_target_properties(zstd::libzstd_shared PROPERTIES
    IMPORTED_LOCATION           "${ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY}"
    INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR}"
  )
endif()

if(NOT TARGET zstd::libzstd_static AND ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)
  add_library(zstd::libzstd_static STATIC IMPORTED GLOBAL)
  set_target_properties(zstd::libzstd_static PROPERTIES
    IMPORTED_LOCATION           "${ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY}"
    INTERFACE_INCLUDE_DIRECTORIES "${ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR}"
  )
endif()

content.thinking:
Wait, the FindZSTD.cmake already has a fallback on line 47-49: if no static library but shared is found, it sets `ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY` to the shared library. And line 52-56 only requires `ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR` and `ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY` for find_package_handle_standard_args.

But the error says `Could NOT find ZSTD (missing: ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)`. Wait, let me re-read the error message more carefully:

```
Could NOT find ZSTD (missing: ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY)


```

But our find_package_handle_standard_args only checks for `ZSTD_INCLUDE_DIR` and `ZSTD_SHARED_LIBRARY`. So this error shouldn't come from our current FindZSTD.cmake!

Oh wait - the file shown is the *current working directory* version which has already been modified. But the CI is running the code from the `unify-backends` branch. The version of FindZSTD.cmake that's on the branch might be different from what's in the working tree. The git status showed `modified: cmake/FindZSTD.cmake` as an unstaged change.

So the branch's committed version must still be checking for `ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY` in find_package_handle_standard_args, which is why the CI is failing with that specific error.

```

content.text: Exactly — the committed version still requires `ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY` as a mandatory variable. The fix (fallback to shared lib + remove from required list) is uncommitted. Let me commit it:

fix: FindZSTD falls back to shared lib when static is unavailable

Conda/pixi environments don't ship libzstd.a. Fall back to the shared
library and remove ZSTD_STATIC_LIBRARY from required variables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
project(pj_bridge)

# Compiler settings
if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Sanitizer options
option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_TSAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
endif()

option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_ASAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
endif()

# Custom cmake modules
list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)

# ============================================================================
# Backend detection
# ============================================================================
find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
# RTI Connext DDS — only look for it if explicitly requested or available
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found — will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found — will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found — skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Common dependencies (for app layer)
# ============================================================================
find_package(ZSTD REQUIRED)

# IXWebSocket (vendored in 3rdparty/)
option(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET "Use vendored IXWebSocket library" ON)
if(USE_VENDORED_IXWEBSOCKET)
  message(STATUS "Using vendored IXWebSocket (3rdparty/ixwebsocket)")
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  add_subdirectory(3rdparty/ixwebsocket)
else()
  find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)
endif()

# spdlog — prefer system package (required for ROS2 ABI compatibility with
# librcl_logging_spdlog), fall back to FetchContent for standalone builds.
include(FetchContent)
find_package(spdlog QUIET)
if(NOT spdlog_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "System spdlog not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    spdlog
    URL https://github.com/gabime/spdlog/archive/refs/tags/v1.15.0.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=9962648c9b4f1a7bbc76fd8d9172555bad1871fdb14ff4f842ef87949682caa5
  )
  set(SPDLOG_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(spdlog)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system spdlog ${spdlog_VERSION}")
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Data path for tests
# ============================================================================
get_filename_component(DATA_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/DATA" ABSOLUTE)
configure_file(data_path.hpp.in "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include/data_path.hpp" @ONLY)
add_library(data_path INTERFACE IMPORTED)
target_include_directories(data_path INTERFACE ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include)

# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_app — backend-agnostic core library
# ============================================================================
add_library(pj_bridge_app STATIC
  app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp
  app/src/message_buffer.cpp
  app/src/session_manager.cpp
  app/src/message_serializer.cpp
  app/src/bridge_server.cpp
  app/src/standalone_event_loop.cpp
)

file.content:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
project(pj_bridge)

# Compiler settings
if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Sanitizer options
option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_TSAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
endif()

option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_ASAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
endif()

# Custom cmake modules
list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)

# ============================================================================
# Backend detection
# ============================================================================
find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
# RTI Connext DDS — only look for it if explicitly requested or available
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found — will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found — will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found — skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Common dependencies (for app layer)
# ============================================================================
find_package(ZSTD REQUIRED)

# IXWebSocket — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)

# spdlog — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(spdlog REQUIRED)

# nlohmann/json — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(nlohmann_json REQUIRED)

# ============================================================================
# Data path for tests
# ============================================================================
get_filename_component(DATA_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/DATA" ABSOLUTE)
configure_file(data_path.hpp.in "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include/data_path.hpp" @ONLY)
add_library(data_path INTERFACE IMPORTED)
target_include_directories(data_path INTERFACE ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include)

# ============================================================================

input.new_string:
include(FetchContent)

# IXWebSocket — try system/conda first, fall back to FetchContent
find_package(ixwebsocket QUIET)
if(NOT ixwebsocket_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "IXWebSocket not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    ixwebsocket
    URL https://github.com/machinezone/IXWebSocket/archive/refs/tags/v11.4.6.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=571b4e88063747ea27f07c58964a4a3c24e27e6bd55dad2c542e3cc41e2f1e16
  )
  set(USE_TLS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_ZLIB ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(IXWEBSOCKET_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(ixwebsocket)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system IXWebSocket")
endif()

# spdlog — prefer system/conda (required for ROS2 ABI compat), FetchContent fallback
find_package(spdlog QUIET)
if(NOT spdlog_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "spdlog not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    spdlog
    URL https://github.com/gabime/spdlog/archive/refs/tags/v1.15.0.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=9962648c9b4f1a7bbc76fd8d9172555bad1871fdb14ff4f842ef87949682caa5
  )
  set(SPDLOG_INSTALL OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(spdlog)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system spdlog ${spdlog_VERSION}")
endif()

# nlohmann/json — try system/conda first, fall back to FetchContent
find_package(nlohmann_json QUIET)
if(NOT nlohmann_json_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "nlohmann_json not found — fetching via FetchContent")
  FetchContent_Declare(
    nlohmann_json
    URL https://github.com/nlohmann/json/archive/refs/tags/v3.11.3.tar.gz
    URL_HASH SHA256=0d8ef5af7f9794e3263480193c491549b2ba6cc74bb018906202ada498a79406
  )
  set(JSON_BuildTests OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(JSON_Install OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  FetchContent_MakeAvailable(nlohmann_json)
else()
  message(STATUS "Using system nlohmann_json ${nlohmann_json_VERSION}")
endif()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.14)
project(pj_bridge)

# Compiler settings
if(CMAKE_COMPILER_IS_GNUCXX OR CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "Clang")
  add_compile_options(-Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
endif()

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

# Sanitizer options
option(ENABLE_TSAN "Enable Thread Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_TSAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=thread -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=thread)
endif()

option(ENABLE_ASAN "Enable Address Sanitizer" OFF)
if(ENABLE_ASAN)
  add_compile_options(-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer -g -O1)
  add_link_options(-fsanitize=address)
endif()

# Custom cmake modules
list(APPEND CMAKE_MODULE_PATH ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake)

# ============================================================================
# Backend detection
# ============================================================================
find_package(ament_cmake QUIET)
# RTI Connext DDS — only look for it if explicitly requested or available
option(ENABLE_RTI "Build RTI Connext DDS backend" OFF)

if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  message(STATUS "ROS2 (ament_cmake) found — will build pj_bridge_ros2")
endif()

if(ENABLE_RTI)
  find_package(RTIConnextDDS QUIET)
  if(RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
    message(STATUS "RTI Connext DDS found — will build pj_bridge_rti")
  else()
    message(WARNING "ENABLE_RTI=ON but RTIConnextDDS not found — skipping RTI backend")
  endif()
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Common dependencies (for app layer)
# ============================================================================
find_package(ZSTD REQUIRED)

# IXWebSocket — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(ixwebsocket REQUIRED)

# spdlog — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(spdlog REQUIRED)

# nlohmann/json — provided by pixi (conda-forge) or Conan
find_package(nlohmann_json REQUIRED)

# ============================================================================
# Data path for tests
# ============================================================================
get_filename_component(DATA_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/DATA" ABSOLUTE)
configure_file(data_path.hpp.in "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include/data_path.hpp" @ONLY)
add_library(data_path INTERFACE IMPORTED)
target_include_directories(data_path INTERFACE ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/include)

# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_app — backend-agnostic core library
# ============================================================================
add_library(pj_bridge_app STATIC
  app/src/middleware/websocket_middleware.cpp
  app/src/message_buffer.cpp
  app/src/session_manager.cpp
  app/src/message_serializer.cpp
  app/src/bridge_server.cpp
  app/src/standalone_event_loop.cpp
)

target_include_directories(pj_bridge_app PUBLIC
  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/app/include
  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/3rdparty
)

target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_app PUBLIC
  zstd::libzstd_shared
  ixwebsocket::ixwebsocket
  spdlog::spdlog
  nlohmann_json::nlohmann_json
)

# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_ros2 — ROS2 backend (only if ament_cmake found)
# ============================================================================
if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  find_package(ament_index_cpp REQUIRED)
  find_package(rclcpp REQUIRED)
  find_package(sensor_msgs REQUIRED)
  find_package(nav_msgs REQUIRED)

  # ROS2 plugin library (sources shared between executable and tests)
  add_library(pj_bridge_ros2_lib STATIC
    ros2/src/topic_discovery.cpp
    ros2/src/schema_extractor.cpp
    ros2/src/generic_subscription_manager.cpp
    ros2/src/message_stripper.cpp
    ros2/src/ros2_topic_source.cpp
    ros2/src/ros2_subscription_manager.cpp
  )

  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/ros2/include
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app
  )

  ament_target_dependencies(pj_bridge_ros2_lib PUBLIC
    ament_index_cpp
    rclcpp
    sensor_msgs
    nav_msgs
  )

  # ROS2 executable
  add_executable(pj_bridge_ros2
    ros2/src/main.cpp
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_ros2
    pj_bridge_ros2_lib
  )

  ament_target_dependencies(pj_bridge_ros2
    rclcpp
  )

  # Install ROS2 executable
  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_ros2
    DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME}
  )
endif()

# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_rti — RTI DDS backend (disabled — replaced by FastDDS backend)
# ============================================================================
# if(ENABLE_RTI AND RTIConnextDDS_FOUND)
#   find_package(CLI11 REQUIRED)
#
#   add_library(pj_bridge_rti_lib STATIC
#     rti/src/dds_topic_discovery.cpp

#   target_include_directories(pj_bridge_rti_lib PUBLIC

#

#   target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_rti pj_bridge_rti_lib CLI11::CLI11)
#   install(TARGETS pj_bridge_rti DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME})
# endif()

# ============================================================================
# pj_bridge_fastdds — eProsima Fast DDS backend (Conan-managed)
# ============================================================================
option(ENABLE_FASTDDS "Build eProsima FastDDS backend" OFF)

if(ENABLE_FASTDDS)
  find_package(fastcdr REQUIRED)
  find_package(fastdds REQUIRED)
  find_package(CLI11 REQUIRED)

  add_library(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib STATIC
    fastdds/src/fastdds_topic_source.cpp
    fastdds/src/fastdds_subscription_manager.cpp
  )

  target_include_directories(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib PUBLIC
    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/fastdds/include
  )

  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_fastdds_lib PUBLIC
    pj_bridge_app
    fastdds
    fastcdr
  )

  add_executable(pj_bridge_fastdds fastdds/src/main.cpp)
  target_link_libraries(pj_bridge_fastdds pj_bridge_fastdds_lib CLI11::CLI11)
  install(TARGETS pj_bridge_fastdds DESTINATION lib/${PROJECT_NAME})
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Install common
# ============================================================================
install(
  DIRECTORY app/include/
  DESTINATION include
)

install(
  TARGETS pj_bridge_app
  EXPORT ${PROJECT_NAME}_appTargets
  ARCHIVE DESTINATION lib
  LIBRARY DESTINATION lib
  RUNTIME DESTINATION bin
)

# Install launch files (if they exist)
if(EXISTS ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/launch)
  install(
    DIRECTORY launch/
    DESTINATION share/${PROJECT_NAME}/launch
  )
endif()

# ============================================================================
# Testing
# ============================================================================
if(BUILD_TESTING AND ament_cmake_FOUND)
  find_package(ament_cmake_gtest REQUIRED)

  ament_add_gtest(${PROJECT_NAME}_tests
    tests/unit/test_websocket_middleware.cpp
    tests/unit/test_message_buffer.cpp
    tests/unit/test_session_manager.cpp
    tests/unit/test_message_serializer.cpp
    tests/unit/test_bridge_server.cpp
    tests/unit/test_protocol_constants.cpp
    tests/unit/test_topic_discovery.cpp
    tests/unit/test_schema_extractor.cpp
    tests/unit/test_generic_subscription_manager.cpp
    tests/unit/test_message_stripper.cpp
  )

  target_link_libraries(${PROJECT_NAME}_tests
    pj_bridge_ros2_lib
    data_path
  )

  ament_target_dependencies(${PROJECT_NAME}_tests
    rclcpp
    sensor_msgs
    nav_msgs
  )

  # Sanitizer environment settings for CTest
  if(ENABLE_TSAN)
    set_tests_properties(${PROJECT_NAME}_tests PROPERTIES
      ENVIRONMENT "TSAN_OPTIONS=suppressions=${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/tsan_suppressions.txt")
  endif()

  if(ENABLE_ASAN)
    set_tests_properties(${PROJECT_NAME}_tests PROPERTIES
      ENVIRONMENT "ASAN_OPTIONS=new_delete_type_mismatch=0;LSAN_OPTIONS=suppressions=${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/asan_suppressions.txt")
  endif()
endif()

# ============================================================================
# ament package (must come last)
# ============================================================================
if(ament_cmake_FOUND)
  ament_package()
endif()
